entries.summary: Hasina compared protesters to razakars at press conference, inflaming public anger.
Students issued one-point demand for Hasina's immediate resignation at Shaheed Minar rally.
Security forces under Hasina's government killed nearly 100 in deadliest single day.
Hasina resigned and fled to India by helicopter after weeks of unrest.
Army chief Waker-uz-Zaman addressed nation announcing Hasina's departure from office.
World leaders publicly reacted to Hasina's removal from power in Bangladesh.
Al Jazeera analysed her downfall as the consequence of one critical misstep.
Bangladesh said it would seek Hasina's extradition from India to face charges.
Bangladesh court issued an arrest warrant for former prime minister Hasina.
Former Awami League student-wing members described as Bangladesh's new outcastes by Al Jazeera.
Hasina's former ministers faced massacre charges as probe deadline was set.
Leaked audio recording revealed Hasina ordered security forces to fire on protesters.
Awami League supporters protested across Bangladesh over crackdown and pending trial.
International Crimes Tribunal convicted Hasina in absentia and sentenced her to death.
Al Jazeera analysed why India is unlikely to return Hasina for execution.
Al Jazeera asked whether Bangladesh would repeat Hasina-era mistakes after conviction.
Killing of a 2024 uprising activist triggered fresh unrest across Bangladesh.
Hasina resigned and fled to India by helicopter after weeks of unrest.
Crowds stormed and looted Hasina's official residence after her departure.
Bangladesh president dissolved parliament following Hasina's resignation and flight.
CNN explainer detailed how protests forced Hasina from office after 15 years.
Students rallied at vandalised Hasina mural demanding accountability for protest killings.
Bangladesh authorities opened the first murder case against the former prime minister.
Bangladesh court issued an arrest warrant for former prime minister Hasina.
Thousands rallied at the March for Unity in Dhaka marking the uprising.
Activists demolished her father Mujibur Rahman's historic Dhaka home as legacy target.
UN human rights office reported up to 1,400 killed during the crackdown.
CNN reported on the uncertain future of Hasina's Awami League party.
Bangladesh tribunal sentenced Hasina to imprisonment in absentia in initial proceeding.
Tribunal indicted Hasina for the killings of protesters during the 2024 uprising.
International Crimes Tribunal convicted Hasina in absentia and imposed the death sentence.
Bangladesh's foreign ministry demanded India hand Hasina over without delay.
Corruption trial began involving Hasina's UK Labour MP niece Tulip Siddiq.
